title: Past Perfect
usage: Одно действие произошло раньше другого в прошлом

rules.example: I had eaten before he arrived.

examples.dialogue: "I had never told anyone about them before."
examples.translation: Я никогда никому об этом не рассказывал.

videoQuiz1.text:
“It was a Thursday afternoon. We had made a pact.”
Did they make the pact on that Thursday afternoon, or before that afternoon arrived?
videoQuiz1.explanation:
When the speaker says, “It was a Thursday (Past Simple)… we had made a pact (Past Perfect),” he is looking back from that Thursday to an even earlier time. The Past Perfect acts like a “super past,” indicating the agreement was already in place before the story began.
She had already eaten lunch before he arrived.

The speaker is using past tenses.
“It was Thursday afternoon. We had made a pact. Your mother had just received her first Harper Avery nomination. She was so excited.”

videoQuiz1.text:
“Your mother had just received her nomination. She was so excited. And I was jealous.”
Did the nomination happen first, or did the speaker’s jealousy happen first?
videoQuiz1.explanation:
We use the Past Perfect to show which of two past actions happened first.
FIRST Your mother had just received her nomination and THEN I was jealous.

videoQuiz1.text:
Compare these two sentences:
A: She received a nomination.
B: She had received a nomination.
Which sentence simply states a past fact without connecting it to another past event?
videoQuiz1.explanation: The Past Simple is a straightforward “point” on a timeline. It tells us what happened, but it doesn’t necessarily link it to the timing of other events.

videoQuiz1.text: Which sentence shows that the action was already finished by the time the other action started?
videoQuiz1.explanation: The Past Perfect creates a “background” effect and shows that the action was even before another action.

videoQuiz1.text:
Look at the sentences:
We had made a pact.
Your mother had just received her nomination.
I hadn’t yet accomplished.
What auxiliary (helping) verb is used in every sentence?
videoQuiz1.explanation:
The auxiliary verb in Past Perfect is always had, regardless of the subject.
I had prepared dinner when my mom came from work.
She had received a nomination.

videoQuiz1.text: Does the auxiliary verb “had” change if the subject is “I”, “We”, or “She”?
videoQuiz1.explanation:
Unlike the Present Perfect (have/has), the Past Perfect always uses had regardless of the subject.
I had prepared dinner when my mom came from work.
She had received a nomination.

videoQuiz1.text: What form is the main verb (made, received, accomplished) in?
videoQuiz1.explanation: All “Perfect” tenses need verbs in the Past Participle. We add -ed/-d to regular verbs (receive → received), irregular verbs change their form (make → made → made).

videoQuiz1.text: Look at the sentence: “Everything I hadn’t yet accomplished.”
a) What word is added to “had” to make the sentence negative?
videoQuiz1.explanation:
To negate the Past Perfect, we attach not to the auxiliary verb: had not or the contraction hadn’t.
I had not finished my meal.
He hadn’t completed the form.

videoQuiz1.text: In the sentence “Your mother had just received her nomination,” what does the word “just” imply?
options: It happened a long time before Thursday.
It happened a very short time before the Thursday.
videoQuiz1.explanation:
“Just” is used to emphasize that the past action occurred very recently before the second past action.
I had just finished cleaning when she came into the room.

videoQuiz1.text: a) Put the words in the correct order:
videoQuiz1.correct: They had made the pact before that day.

videoQuiz1.text: b) Put the words in the correct order:
videoQuiz1.correct: He had not accomplished anything yet.

blocks.intro:
We use Past Perfect for:
  • completed action before another action in the past;
  • completed action before a specific time;
  • states continuing up to a past moment.
We often use Past Perfect adverbs just, already to emphasise how long ago the first event had happened.

The word order for positive Past Perfect sentences is SAV3
(Subject + had + Past Participle).
The auxiliary verb is the same for all subjects.
SubjectAuxiliary VerbMain Verb (V3/past participle)
Ihadasked
Youhadasked
He / She / Ithadasked
Wehadasked
Theyhadasked
examples: They had made a pact before that day.
She had just received a nomination when I saw her.
I had finished my work by 5 PM yesterday.
He had felt tired until he saw his daughter.

blocks.intro:
Negative Sentences
The word order for negative Past Perfect sentences is SAV3
Subject + hadn’t + V3 (past participle).
We also can use “never” in negative sentences to say that something happened zero times in the past.
Contraction: hadn’t = had not.

We use “yet” to emphasise that the action was expected to happen before a specific time but not completed.
examples: They had never seen that movie before last night.
He had not finished the report when the meeting started.

blocks.intro:
V3 Reference (Irregular Verbs)
For regular verbs, add -ed. For irregular verbs, use the 3rd form (Past Participle).
Base (V1)Past Simple (V2)Past Participle (V3)
Makemademade
Gowentgone
Seesawseen
Dodiddone
Havehadhad
Base (V1)Past Simple (V2)Past Participle (V3)
Givegavegiven
Eatateeaten
Comecamecome
Taketooktaken
Writewrotewritten
Knowknewknown
Thinkthoughtthought
Speakspokespoken
Findfoundfound
Runranrun
Becomebecamebecome
Beginbeganbegun
Breakbrokebroken
Bringbroughtbrought
Buyboughtbought
Choosechosechosen
Drivedrovedriven
Fallfellfallen
Feelfeltfelt
Forgetforgotforgotten
Getgotgotten / got
Growgrewgrown
Hearheardheard

videoQuiz1b.text: Richard and Meredith's mother ______ a pact before things changed.
videoQuiz1b.explanation: had made — Past Perfect: had + Past Participle. make → made

videoQuiz1b.text: By that Thursday afternoon, Meredith's mother ______ her first Harper Avery nomination.
videoQuiz1b.explanation: had received — Past Perfect: had + Past Participle. receive → received

videoQuiz1b.text: Richard realized that she ______ far ahead of him in her career.
Before he felt jealous, they ______ to leave their partners for each other.
videoQuiz1b.explanation: had decided — Past Perfect: had + Past Participle. decide → decided

videoQuiz1b.text: Her success illuminated everything that Richard ______ yet.
videoQuiz1b.explanation: had accomplished — Past Perfect: had + Past Participle. accomplish → accomplished

videoQuiz1b.text: By the time they talked, she ______ very excited about her nomination.
Richard felt that she ______ too far ahead to catch up to.
videoQuiz1b.explanation: had stepped — Past Perfect: had + Past Participle. step → stepped

videoQuiz1b.text: Before that day, Richard ______ a strong connection with her.
He admitted that his jealousy ______ from a hateful place.
The situation ______ clear to him only after her success.

videoQuiz2.text: Focus on Elle Woods’ lines: “What had you done earlier that day?” and “Had you ever gotten a perm before?”
a) When Elle asks “What had you done earlier that day?”, she is asking about:
options: What the witness is doing right now
What the witness was doing or did when the crime happened
What the witness had done the day before the crime happened
videoQuiz2.explanation: Past Perfect looks back from a past moment to an even earlier time — the action happened before another past event.

videoQuiz2.text: b) In the question “Had you ever gotten a perm before?” Elle is asking about:
options: If the witness got a perm before the crime happened
If the witness got a perm in her life overall
videoQuiz2.explanation: She isn’t asking if the witness has ever had a perm in her entire life up until now (Present Perfect); she is asking if the witness had experience with perms before the day the crime happened.

videoQuiz2.text: Look at the sentence: “Had you ever gotten a perm before?”
a) What is the first word of the question?
videoQuiz2.explanation: In English, we create questions by moving the auxiliary verb to the front. For Past Perfect, that word is always had.

videoQuiz2.text: b) The subject (“you”) in interrogative sentences stays?
options: Before the auxiliary verb (He had eaten?)
After the auxiliary verb (Had he eaten?)
videoQuiz2.explanation:
In questions, auxiliary verbs and subjects change their places.
Had you gotten a perm before the crime?
Had she entered the house before she heard the gunshot?

videoQuiz2.text: Look at the sentence: “What had you done earlier that day?”
a) When we use a question word (What, Where, Why) in interrogative sentences, it stays…
options: At the beginning of the sentence
videoQuiz2.explanation:
Question words (WH- words) always take the “priority position” at the start of a sentence, even before the auxiliary verb.
Example: What had you done earlier that day?

videoQuiz2.text: In the sentence “Had you ever gotten a perm before?”

options: At any time before the crime.
At any time in the witness’s life.
videoQuiz2.explanation:
We use “ever” to talk about the “experience” within a time frame. In Past Perfect, that time frame is “any time in your life up until a specific past event.”
Had you ever tried alcohol before your 21st birthday party?
We use “never” for negative statements; “ever” is the standard way to ask if an experience existed at any point in time.

videoQuiz2.text: Put the words in the correct order:
Put the words in the correct order for a WH- question:

blocks.intro:
1. Yes/No Questions
The word order for yes/no interrogative Past Perfect sentences is ASV3?
Had + Subject + V3?

We use had or hadn’t for short answers.
examples: “Had you gotten a perm before the crime?”
“Had you seen him before that day?” — “Yes, I had.” / “No, I hadn’t.”

blocks.intro:
2. WH- Questions
The word order for specific questions in Past Perfect is ASV3?
WH- word + had + Subject + V3?
examples: “What had you done before the crime?”

videoQuiz2b.text: Richard was jealous because he ______ as much success as Meredith’s mother by that time.
videoQuiz2b.explanation: hadn't had — negative Past Perfect: hadn't + Past Participle. have → had

videoQuiz2b.text: At that point in his life, Richard ______ any Harper Avery nominations yet.
videoQuiz2b.explanation: hadn't received — negative Past Perfect: hadn't + Past Participle. receive → received

videoQuiz2b.text: Richard realized that he ______ as much in his career as Meredith’s mother.
videoQuiz2b.explanation: hadn't accomplished — negative Past Perfect: hadn't + Past Participle. accomplish → accomplished

videoQuiz2b.text: Before that Thursday, Richard ______ how far ahead Meredith’s mother had moved.
videoQuiz2b.explanation: hadn't noticed — negative Past Perfect: hadn't + Past Participle. notice → noticed

videoQuiz2b.text: By the time of the nomination, Richard ______ how to deal with his jealousy.
videoQuiz2b.explanation: hadn't learned — negative Past Perfect: hadn't + Past Participle. learn → learned

videoQuiz2b.text: What ______ Chutney Wyndham ______ earlier that day before coming home?
videoQuiz2b.explanation: Had / done — Past Perfect question: Had + subject + V3. do → did → done

videoQuiz2b.text: ______ she ______ a latte before she went to the gym?
videoQuiz2b.explanation: Had / gotten — Past Perfect question: Had + subject + V3. get → got → gotten

videoQuiz2b.text: Before Chutney came home, ______ she ______ a perm?
videoQuiz2b.explanation: had / gotten — Past Perfect question: had + subject + V3. getted is not a real word.

videoQuiz2b.text: Why did Elle Woods ask: “______ you ever ______ a perm before?”
videoQuiz2b.explanation: Had / had — Past Perfect question: Had + subject + V3. have → had → had

videoQuiz2b.text: ______ the witness ______ in the shower before the incident happened?
videoQuiz2b.explanation: Had / been — Past Perfect question: Had + subject + V3. be → was/were → been

exercises.question: Он пришел после того, как я закончил работу. Выберите верно:
options: He came after I finished work.
He came after I had finished work.
He comes after I had finished work.
He had come after I finished work.
exercises.explanation: Более раннее действие: Past Perfect (had finished), позже: Past Simple (came).

exercises.question: Дополните: "She __ already left when we arrived."
Выберите правильное предложение:
options: They had gone home before I called.
They go home before I called.
They were going home before I called.
They have gone home before I called.
exercises.explanation: Past Perfect для более раннего действия: had gone.

exercises.question: Выберите отрицание в Past Perfect:
options: I not had finished my homework.
I didn't had finished my homework.
I have not finished my homework.
exercises.explanation: Past Perfect отрицание: had + not + Past Participle.

exercises.question: Дополните отрицание: "He __ not __ the message before noon."
exercises.explanation: Past Perfect отрицание: had not received.

exercises.question: Какой вопрос грамматически верен?
options: Had you ever seen a ghost?
Have you ever seen a ghost?
Did you ever see a ghost?
Had you seen a ghost before?
exercises.explanation: Past Perfect вопрос: Had + подлежащее + Past Participle?

exercises.question: Завершите вопрос: "__ they __ the agreement before the meeting?"
exercises.explanation: Past Perfect вопрос: Had they signed?

exercises.question: Выберите правильное использование двух прошедших времен:
options: By the time he arrived, I finish eating.
By the time he arrived, I had finished eating.
By the time he arrives, I finish eating.
By the time he arrives, I had finished eating.
exercises.explanation: Past Perfect обозначает действие, завершенное до другого действия.

exercises.question: Найдите ошибку:
options: They had already arrived when we got there.
I hadn't met her before that day.
She had finish her work before 5 PM.
We had never been to Paris before.

exercises.question: Выберите правильный вариант:
options: After he had left, she started crying.
After he left, she had started crying.
After he has left, she started crying.
After he leaves, she starts crying.

exercises.question: Какой вариант содержит ошибку?
options: She had never seen snow before.
I had go away before you arrived.

exercises.question: Какое предложение правильно выражает последовательность?
options: He left the room and then I saw the document.
He had left the room and then I saw the document.
He had left the room before I saw the document.
He leaves the room before I see the document.
exercises.explanation: Past Perfect подчеркивает, что первое действие произошло ДО второго.

exercises.question: Какой вопрос требует ответа "Yes, I had"?
options: Had you already finished by midnight?
Are you finished with your work?

exercises.question: Дополните: "By the time she called, I __ __ the email."
exercises.explanation: Past Perfect для действия, завершившегося до другого: had sent.

exercises.question: Выберите правильный порядок слов в Past Perfect вопросе:
options: What had they done before that?
What they had done before that?
Had they what done before that?
They had done what before that?
exercises.explanation: Past Perfect вопрос: Question word + Had + подлежащее + Past Participle?
